Title: Edge shape, dashed, and waypoints
Summary: Click an edge to open a small inline toolbar. Curved or straight, solid or dashed, plus draggable waypoints.
Body:
## The toolbar

Click an edge to select it. A small toolbar appears near the label with four buttons: curved, straight, solid, dashed. Click again to flip a setting. The selection is per edge and persists in the per folder `_layout.json`.

## Waypoints

Double click the edge path (anywhere away from the label) to add a waypoint at the click point. Waypoints render as small emerald squares. Drag a waypoint to bend the edge. Double click a waypoint to remove it.

The path goes through every waypoint in order:

- Curved + waypoints: Catmull Rom smooth curve through all points.
- Straight + waypoints: polyline.

## When to use what

- Default curved + solid is fine 90% of the time.
- Switch to straight when two sequences are physically close and a curve would loop weirdly.
- Switch to dashed for "this branch is rare" or "this is a fallback path" semantics. Bleepforge does not enforce a meaning; pick a convention for your project.
- Use waypoints when an edge would otherwise cross unrelated nodes. One or two waypoints usually clears the cluster.

> tip: dangling edges (pointing to a sequence that does not exist in the current folder) turn red and are always dashed, regardless of your setting. That is enforced visual feedback for "this reference is broken."
